question: Write a C++ program that dynamically allocates a 1D array of integers of a size specified by the user. Fill the array with user-provided values, print all elements, and then deallocate the memory.

test_cases.expected:
Enter the size of the array: Enter 3 integer values:
Enter value for element 0: Enter value for element 1: Enter value for element 2: Array elements: 1 2 3

Enter the size of the array: Invalid input. Please enter a positive integer for the size: Invalid input. Please enter a positive integer for the size: Invalid input. Please enter a positive integer for the size: Enter 4 integer values:
Enter value for element 0: Enter value for element 1: Enter value for element 2: Enter value for element 3: Array elements: 10 20 30 40

Enter the size of the array: Enter 2 integer values:
Enter value for element 0: Invalid input. Please enter an integer: Enter value for element 1: Invalid input. Please enter an integer: Array elements: 10 20

driver: // The program is expected to be compiled and run as a standalone executable.
